description:
Filed retroactively (origin: silent cut in fz-ul4.6, flagged 2026-05-10). See memory/project_polymorphic_interp_jit_dispatch.md.

Problem: Value::Jit is only bound for monomorphic JIT'd fns. Polymorphic fns keep the AST closure in interp — correct but slow, and asymmetric: an interp-only caller invoking id(42) runs interp clauses instead of the matching JIT spec.

Approach:
1. Bind Value::JitPoly { user_name, specs: Vec<(Vec<LowerTy>, JitFn)> } for polymorphic JIT'd fns.
2. Interp::apply for Value::JitPoly inspects each arg's runtime LowerTy shape and dispatches to the matching spec's reverse thunk.
3. Fall back to AST clauses if no spec matches.

Deps: none. Orthogonal cleanup. Heap support (fz-ul4.11) shrinks interp-only-caller set and reduces urgency.

Tests: program where an interp-only fn calls a polymorphic fn with multiple shapes; assert each call goes through the matching JIT spec.

Notes: runtime LowerTy derivation already lives implicitly in value_to_slots — factor it out. Tuple derivation recurses. Keep AST-clause fallback as safety net.
title: Polymorphic interp→JIT dispatcher
